appeal_letter:
Re: Appeal of Denial — Out-of-Network Wrist MRI dated 06/02/2026

Dear Appeals Reviewer,

I am writing to appeal the denial of coverage for the wrist MRI performed on 06/02/2026 at an out-of-network radiology center. This letter asks that the decision be reconsidered based on the urgent clinical circumstances under which the imaging was obtained.

The denial stated that the plan requires prior authorization for all advanced imaging and specifically requires a Network Exception authorization before out-of-network services are rendered. The denial stated that no such authorization was requested or approved, and that because this was not a life-threatening emergency, the member is responsible for the out-of-network charges.

I want to confront that reasoning directly. The denial turns on two points: the absence of a pre-service authorization and the characterization of the situation as not a life-threatening emergency. The clinical context bears directly on both. The member fell while skateboarding and was evaluated at urgent care. X-rays were negative, but the treating clinician specifically suspected a scaphoid fracture. A suspected scaphoid fracture is clinically significant because a missed scaphoid fracture can lead to bone death (avascular necrosis), which is precisely why scaphoid injuries with negative initial X-rays are commonly imaged promptly rather than deferred. The clinician acted on that concern by sending the member immediately to the adjacent imaging center.

On the authorization point, the reason no prior authorization or Network Exception was obtained is the same reason the imaging was clinically appropriate: the referral was urgent and same-day, made directly by the treating clinician at the point of care. The denial treats the missing authorization as a simple administrative failure by the member, but the documented facts show a time-sensitive clinical referral in which the member was directed to the nearest available imaging resource. I ask that the reviewer evaluate the authorization requirement against these individualized facts and treat the urgent, clinician-directed nature of the referral as the basis for a retrospective Network Exception or authorization rather than denying coverage outright.

On the emergency characterization, even accepting that this was not classified as a life-threatening emergency, the standard urgency framework still applies: the risk of avascular necrosis from a missed scaphoid fracture made prompt imaging time-sensitive. I ask that this urgency be weighed in determining whether the pre-service authorization requirement should be applied so as to deny the claim entirely.

I also note a procedural point. The denial as provided does not state the deadline for filing this appeal, the process for submitting it, or the member's right to a full and fair review. I respectfully assert the member's right to a complete and fair review and request that the applicable appeal rights, deadlines, and procedures be provided in writing.

Requested action: I ask that the plan overturn the denial and process the 06/02/2026 wrist MRI as covered, granting a retrospective Network Exception or authorization in light of the urgent, clinician-directed referral; or alternatively provide a written explanation of how a member is expected to obtain pre-service authorization during a same-day urgent referral for a suspected scaphoid fracture.

The referring clinician and urgent care can provide supporting documentation on request.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Urgent care visit note documenting the fall, negative X-rays, and the clinician's suspicion of a scaphoid fracture
The referring clinician's written statement explaining the urgent, same-day referral to the imaging center
Documentation of the date and time of the urgent care visit relative to the MRI to establish same-day urgency
The MRI report and order showing the clinical indication
Plan language defining the Network Exception process and any exception for urgent or clinician-directed referrals
The applicable appeal deadline, filing procedure, and statement of the member's review rights
